claims.text: From 3c = b, dividing both sides by 3 gives c = b / 3, so c + 3 = b / 3 + 3 and the correct choice is E.
claims.why_supported: Two directly opened instructional sources support dividing both sides by 3 to isolate the variable and preserving equality when the same operation is applied on both sides.

sources.title: 1.1 Solving One-Step Linear Equations | Elementary Algebra
sources.copied_text: Divide both sides of the equation by 3 to isolate the variable.
sources.why_this_source_counts: Directly supports the algebra move from 3c = b to c = b / 3.

sources.title: 4.7 Solve Equations with Fractions - Prealgebra | OpenStax
sources.copied_text: When you add or divide the same quantity from both sides of an equation, you still have equality.
sources.why_this_source_counts: Confirms the equality-preserving operations used to derive c = b / 3 and then c + 3.
